query: where can I get food near me
ai_overview: If you're looking for food options nearby, the Yablokoff Wallace Dining Center (YWDC) is a great choice. Located on Scholars Lane near the Joseph E. Gallo Recreation & Wellness Center, it offers a variety of nutritious meals in a modern setting. The YWDC is open to campus residents, commuter students, staff, faculty, and visitors, accepting various payment methods including meal plans and credit cards. Alternatively, the Pavilion is another dining facility inspired by the Central Valley’s agricultural heritage, offering indoor and outdoor seating with a focus on healthy and sustainable food options. For quicker bites, consider visiting Snack Shop 1 located in the first floor library east wing or Snack Shop 2 situated between Cathedral and Tenaya.

ai_overview: Several buildings at UC Merced are dedicated to research activities. Lab 3A, part of the Academic Quad, is a five-story teaching and research building focused on biomedical sciences and physics, completed as part of the 2020 Project campus expansion. Lab 2A, also in the Academic Quad, is a four-story facility designed for sustainability research and engineering, featuring wet/dry laboratories and shared spaces to encourage cross-disciplinary interaction. Science and Engineering 2 provides research and instructional labs and core facilities, with notable outdoor spaces and collaboration areas, and has achieved LEED Platinum certification. The Greenhouse supports controlled plant-growth research in areas such as food and water security and climate change. Additionally, Science and Engineering 1 offers laboratories and support spaces for the Schools of Natural Sciences and Engineering.

query: what services does uc merced offer
ai_overview: UC Merced offers a wide range of services to support its students, faculty, and staff. The Transportation & Parking Services (TAPS) provides safe and reliable parking and transit services, including a transit hub with real-time bus arrival information. The Academic Office Annex offers academic support services such as tutoring and STEM support. The University Police enhance campus safety through high standards of police services and community outreach. Additionally, the Welcome Center offers guided and virtual tours to help prospective students and visitors explore the campus. The Administration Building serves as a hub for student services, including financial aid and registration, while the Early Childhood Education Center provides child care services. Lastly, the Central Plant & Telecommunications Complex supports campus infrastructure with heating, cooling, and telecommunications services.
